what_is_certain: Weapons and shields now share module-local helpers in app_support/dataset_ui.py and app_support/dataset_presentations.py.
The detailed-view and Catalog UI behavior remained unchanged in smoke tests.
The project Conda environment elden_ring_ui has the runtime dependencies needed for verification.
what_remains_uncertain: Whether a later slice should be folded into the same abstraction family or kept separate until its own parity tests are written.
cycles.architectural_intent: Make the equipment catalog pair a canonical shared contract while preserving current resolver paths, labels, and dataset-specific selectors.
changes_made: Extended tests/test_dataset_presentation.py to validate weapons and shields parity.
Added direct render assertions for equipment description and edition output.
validated_assumptions: Weapons and shields are literal near-clones in both registries except for dataset_key and label.
The shared equipment helpers can stay module-local without touching app.py.
exceptions: Selector labels remain explicit in app_support/view_state.py.
Spell-family and progression-table deduplication stay deferred because they are separate semantic families.

commands: conda run -n elden_ring_ui python -m unittest tests.test_dataset_ui_registry tests.test_dataset_presentation tests.test_view_state_helpers

cycles.next_slice_or_question: Apply the same slice discipline to weapons_upgrades and shields_upgrades only if the progression-table contract needs another canonical helper.

what_is_certain: Weapons and shields upgrades now share module-local progression helpers in app_support/dataset_ui.py and app_support/dataset_presentations.py.
The progression-table loader profile now resolves the explicit weapon name and shield name identity columns instead of falling back to id/name.
The armors and progression smoke coverage passed after restoring valid loading-instructions JSON and hardening DataLoader path resolution.
what_remains_uncertain: Whether the remaining top-level dataset families should also be moved onto explicit loader profiles in a later slice or left on the current full-file fallback path.
cycles.architectural_intent: Make progression tables load through a shared column-loading contract while preserving browse-only rendering and explicit title-field differences.
changes_made: Hardened DataLoader path resolution and dataset-specific identity-column overrides.
Extended tests/test_data_loader_cache.py, tests/test_dataset_ui_registry.py, tests/test_dataset_presentation.py, and tests/test_ui_smoke.py for progression parity.
validated_assumptions: The progression-table pair is structurally identical except for the title field and default sort field.
A dataset-specific always_include override is required for progression tables because they do not expose the generic name column.
The loading-instructions contract must be valid JSON for the loader profile path to work at app startup.
exceptions: The progression-table profile uses explicit id plus weapon name or shield name identity columns rather than the generic name field.
Other top-level families still use the current loader fallback and remain outside this slice.

commands: conda run -n elden_ring_ui python -m unittest tests.test_data_loader_cache tests.test_dataset_ui_registry tests.test_dataset_presentation tests.test_ui_smoke

conda run -n elden_ring_ui python -c "from data_loader import DataLoader; print(DataLoader().build_profile_columns('armors','single_piece_visual')); print(DataLoader().build_profile_columns('weapons_upgrades','progression_table_visual'))"

cycles.next_slice_or_question: Decide whether the remaining top-level dataset families should be brought onto explicit loader profiles or left as deliberate fallback cases.
